exemplar.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Balanced distribution reflects the studentâs demonstrated strengths in analysis and coherent argumentation, while accounting for weaker areas of organization and deeper evaluation.
weaknesses: The subdivision into criteria is inferred rather than drawn directly from the official rubric, and numerical maxima per criterion are not specified in the prompt.

annotations.comment: The student directly engages the prescribed title by quoting Boxâs claim in full. This clear framing signals a sustained focus on the title and provides a strong foundation for the subsequent analysis.
The critique of Malthusâs model for failing to anticipate technological and demographic shifts is thorough and shows strong evaluative engagement with its assumptions.
Labeling Malthusâs theory as âentirely misleadingâ is overstated without nuanced discussion of the historical context in which it produced valuable initial insights.
Consider integrating demographic transition theory as a counter-evidence example to deepen the evaluation of population models in the human sciences.
The shift to human sciences with the phrase âBoxâs assertion appliesâ¦â feels abrupt. Add a clearer transition that links the mathematical discussion to the human sciences section.
The exposition of the Phillips Curve and its historical policy use in the 1950sâ60s is detailed and situates the model within its real-world application context.
The critique of the Phillips Curve could be strengthened by citing quantitative stagflation data or case studies to support the claim that it fails under supply-shock conditions.
To deepen evaluation, discuss developments such as the expectations-augmented Phillips Curve and rational expectations critique to show alternative perspectives.
Consider proposing specific methodologies for testing and refining models across diverse contexts to demonstrate forward-looking critical thinking.

The introduction effectively defines models as simplifications built on assumptions and explains why perfect accuracy would negate the purpose of modelling. This demonstrates sound conceptual understanding.

The opening paragraphs lack a concise thesis statement that explicitly states the extent of agreement with Boxâs claim. A clearer stance and roadmap would improve coherence and signal direction to the reader.
This sentence fragment ends abruptly, indicating a missing transition into the discussion of axioms. Ensure that each paragraph introduces its focus fully before moving to the next idea.

The discussion of Euclidean versus non-Euclidean geometry, linked to Einsteinâs general relativity, is an effective, discipline-specific example that illustrates both usefulness and wrongness of models in mathematics.
While the example is well chosen, the paragraph does not probe the epistemic status of axioms themselvesâwhy certain axioms are acceptedâand how that shapes the notion of a âwrongâ model.
The explanation of Euclidean geometryâs practical use in architecture and graphics despite theoretical limitations demonstrates effective analysis of context-dependence in model utility.
The Malthusian example is introduced effectively, but the essay asserts it is entirely misleading without engaging how proponents might defend the model or contextualize its original empirical basis.
Introducing the Quantity Theory of Money as a counter-example effectively demonstrates awareness of models that defy the universality of Boxâs claim.
The equation MV=PT appears in MathML rather than LaTeX. It should be formatted as $$MV = PT$$ to align with IB expectations and improve clarity.
Using the Zimbabwe hyperinflation case effectively illustrates the predictive power of the Quantity Theory of Money in a concrete context.
The discussion notes constant velocity as a simplification but does not consider empirical measures of V or alternative formulations, missing an opportunity for deeper critique.
The conclusion repeats earlier points about usefulness and wrongness. Instead, synthesise the implications of each example to offer a more integrated final evaluation.
The discussion of broader implicationsâcritical evaluation of assumptions and need for ongoing refinementâdemonstrates the studentâs awareness of how knowledge evolves.
Recommendations for refinement are presented too generally; link them back to specific examples (e.g., how Euclidean geometry axioms might adapt) for stronger integration.
The final paragraph restates prior analysis without adding new insight. A more evaluative closing should reflect on the implications of exceptions to Boxâs claim.
The works cited list is consistently formatted but omits page numbers for quoted print sources and access dates for web references. Ensure complete citation details for academic precision.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
